response.justification: The role's hard minimum of 8 years C++ experience is essentially unmet: the candidate's C++ usage appears limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (~6 months, C/C++ for a desktop IDE) and a student thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in the résumé's own skills section. The candidate's 15+ year career is primarily built on JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js for web and SaaS products, with no evidence of production cloud compute platform ownership, no cloud architecture credentials, and no formal technical-leadership role over cloud or infrastructure engineers. An active career gap since October 2025 adds further risk.
strengths: Genuine high-throughput distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O, real-time metadata pipelines)
Consistent software-craftsmanship culture: TDD, trunk-based dev, CI/CD, mentoring via public workshops and talks
Broad polyglot background (DLang, TypeScript, Swift, Java, Docker, Kubernetes) shows adaptability across layers of the stack
concerns: C++ experience does not meet the 8-year minimum; it is absent from the candidate's own skills list and appears only in a ~6-month role and a thesis project
No cloud compute platform ownership or production cloud architecture experience at scale; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is tooling-level, not platform-level
No demonstrated technical-leadership tenure over cloud or infrastructure teams; staff/senior IC roles do not substitute for the required 2+ years leading DevOps/cloud engineers
